text: A traditional stew made with garbanzo beans, cabbage, carrots, and pork. Slow-cooked and seasoned with herbs, it’s a comforting dish rooted in local home cooking.
Crispy wheat snacks topped with spicy salsa, lime juice, and salt. A crunchy, tangy street food favorite that’s perfect for snacking while exploring the town.

Dolores Hidalgo is famous for its exotic ice cream flavors — from tequila and avocado to rose petal and mole. These handmade treats are a must-try, offering a playful and delicious taste of local creativity.
